'''Eriscot''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Waterworld/Legends|water world]] located in the [[Haserian sector/Legends|Haserian sector]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Nejma]] [[Species/Legends|species]], which was relocated to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] by a [[Unidentified spacefaring benefactors|group]] of [[Realspace/Legends|spacefaring]] benefactors after a series of cataclysms on several [[Water/Legends|water]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], including Eriscot, five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. During the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[Sentientologist|anthropologist]] [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Mammon Hoole|Hoole]] was studying the ruins of those worlds.

==Description==
Eriscot was a [[Waterworld/Legends|water world]]<ref name="Gamer1" /> located in the [[Eriscot system/Legends|Eriscot system]], a part of the [[Haserian sector/Legends|Haserian sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
[[File:Nejma.jpg|thumb|right|185px|Eriscot was the homeworld of the Nejma species (foreground).]]
Eriscot was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Nejma]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]].<ref name="Gamer1" /> At some point five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Date">{{CSWECite|II|119|Iskalonian}} states that the [[Species/Legends|species]] constituting the [[Iskalonian]] cultural group were brought to [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] approximately five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Since "[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series]]" establishes that the relocation of the Iskalonian species was prefaced by the cataclysms on the species' original [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]], the cataclysms must also have occurred around that [[Time/Legends|time]].</ref> a series of cataclysms occurred on several water [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including Eriscot. The catastrophes left the native inhabitants helpless until a [[Unidentified spacefaring benefactors|group]] of [[Realspace/Legends|spacefaring]] benefactors arrived and helped relocate the species of the affected worlds to<ref name="Gamer1" /> the Mid Rim<ref name="EAOC Iskalon">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Iskalon system/Legends|Iskalon system]]</ref> planet [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]], where the latter eventually formed the [[Iskalonian]] cultural group.<ref name="Gamer1" />

At some point during the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[Shi'ido]] [[Sentientologist|anthropologist]] [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Mammon Hoole|Hoole]] was studying the ruins of the Iskalonian homeworlds, including Eriscot, to determine the nature of the cataclysms that occurred there and the identity of the Iskalonian benefactors.<ref name="Gamer1" /> Eriscot was also eventually mentioned in an entry dedicated to the Iskalonians in the [[Book/Legends|book]] ''[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'', which was based on Hoole's notes and published during the New Republic's rule.<ref name="TEGAS">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Eriscot was first mentioned in the article "[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series]]," which was authored by [[Rich Handley]] and [[Joseph Bongiorno|Joe Bongiorno]] and published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 1|first issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Gamer1" /> in [[November]] [[2000]].<ref name="GamerDate">''[[Star Wars Gamer Special Edition]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Eriscot [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square Q-14.<ref name="TEA" />
